stem: A industrial engineering study measures the assembly time (seconds) for a sample of 25 factory workers. The sample mean is 252.6 sec with a sample standard deviation of 52.5 sec. However, 2 of the 25 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 23 observations have a mean of 246.6 sec and standard deviation of 32.3 sec. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 252.6, 95% CI = [230.9, 274.3]. Without outliers: mean = 246.6, 95% CI = [233.1, 260.1]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [230.9, 274.3]. Point estimate: 249.6 sec.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [230.9, 274.3].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
